title: FINRA Case 23-02063 — Richard Hall, Sharon Hall v. Darien Bonney
text:
Award
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
In the Matter of the Arbitration Between:
Claimants Case Number: 23-02063
Richard Hall and Sharon Hall
vs.
Respondents Hearing Site: Phoenix, Arizona
MML Investors Services, LLC and
Darien Bonney
Awards are rendered by independent arbitrators who are chosen by the parties to issue final,
binding decisions. FINRA makes available an arbitration forum—pursuant to rules approved by
the SEC—but has no part in deciding the award.
Nature of the Dispute: Customers vs. Member and Associated Person
This case was decided by an all-public panel.
REPRESENTATION OF PARTIES
For Claimants Richard Hall and Sharon Hall (collectively, “Claimants”): Lars K. Soreide, Esq.,
Soreide Law Group, Pompano Beach, Florida.*
For Respondent MML Investors Services, LLC (“MML”): Julie E. Ravis, Esq., Stevens & Lee,
Reading, Pennsylvania.
For Respondent Darien Bonney (“Bonney”): Arno T. Naeckel, Esq., Patented PLLC, Scottsdale,
Arizona
Hereinafter, MML and Bonney are collectively referred to as “Respondents”.
*FINRA recorded the appearance of Claimants’ counsel at the time of filing of the Statement of
Claim. Counsel’s representation of Claimants may have ended with the parties’ settlement.
CASE INFORMATION
Statement of Claim filed on or about: July 26, 2023.
Amended Statement of Claim filed on or about: October 23, 2023.
Sharon Hall signed the Submission Agreement: November 13, 2023.
Richard Hall signed the Submission Agreement: November 13, 2023.
Statement of Answer filed by MML on or about: October 2, 2023.
Statement of Answer to Amended Statement of Claim filed by MML on or about: November 28,

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 23-02063
Award Page 2 of 6
2023.
MML signed the Submission Agreement: November 28, 2023.
Statement of Answer filed by Bonney on or about: September 27, 2024.
Bonney did not sign the Submission Agreement.
CASE SUMMARY
In the Statement of Claim, as amended, Claimants asserted the following causes of action
against Respondents: negligence and breach of fiduciary duty. In addition, Claimants asserted a
cause of action for negligent supervision against MML. The causes of action relate to
unspecified securities.
Unless specifically admitted in the Statement of Answer to Amended Statement of Claim, MML
denied the allegations made in the Statement of Claim, as amended, and asserted various
affirmative defenses.
Unless specifically admitted in the Statement of Answer, Bonney denied the allegations made in
the Statement of Claim, as amended, and asserted various affirmative defenses.
RELIEF REQUESTED
In the Statement of Claim, as amended, Claimants requested: an award for damages in the
amount of $225,000.00, attorneys’ fees, punitive damages, interest, costs, and any further relief
the Panel deems just and proper.
In the Statement of Answer to Amended Statement of Claim, MML requested: the Panel dismiss
the Amended Statement of Claim with prejudice and/or deny any award to Claimants.
In the Statement of Answer, Bonney requested: the Panel dismiss the Amended Statement of
Claim and Bonney recover costs and expenses incurred herein, including attorneys’ fees.
Bonney filed a request for expungement of all references to Occurrence Number 2286843 from
Central Registration Depository (“CRD”) registration records (CRD Number 4899007). Please
see the Other Issues Considered and Decided section of this Award for more information.
OTHER ISSUES CONSIDERED AND DECIDED
The Arbitrators acknowledge that they have each read the pleadings and other materials filed by
the parties.
Bonney did not file a properly executed Submission Agreement but is required to submit to
arbitration pursuant to the Code of Arbitration Procedure (“Code”) and, having answered the
claim, is bound by the determination of the Panel on all issues submitted.
On October 23, 2023, Claimants submitted a Motion to Amend the Statement of Claim to add
Bonney as an additional respondent (“Motion to Amend”). On November 2, 2023, MML
submitted its non-opposition to the Motion to Amend. On November 6, 2023, the Chairperson on

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 23-02063
Award Page 3 of 6
behalf of the Panel issued an order to notify Bonney of the November 9, 2023 pre-hearing
conference to hear oral arguments on the Motion to Amend. On that same day, the notification
of the pre-hearing conference was sent to Bonney. On November 9, 2023, the Panel heard oral
argument on the Motion to Amend. On that same day, the Panel granted the Motion to Amend.
On October 15, 2024, Claimants filed a notice of settlement and voluntary dismissal with
prejudice against MML. Therefore, the Panel made no determination with respect to any of the
relief requests contained in the Statement of Claim, as amended, with regard to MML. The case
continued to proceed with Claimants’ claims against remaining respondent, Bonney.
On October 29, 2024, Claimants filed a Motion to Compel and for Sanctions, to which no
response was filed. In its Order dated November 19, 2024, the Panel denied the motion.
On February 13, 2026, Claimants filed a notice of voluntary dismissal with prejudice against
Bonney, but requested that the case remain open for Bonney to seek expungement. Claimants
did not oppose Bonney’s request for expungement. Therefore, the Panel made no determination
with respect to any of the relief requests contained in the Statement of Claim, as amended, with
regard to Bonney.
On February 18, 2026, Bonney filed a Motion for Expungement, to which no response was filed.
No hearing on Bonney’s expungement request was held; the Panel decided the request for
expungement on the papers.
AWARD
After considering the pleadings, the Panel has decided in full and final resolution of the issues
submitted for determination as follows:
1. Respondent Darien Euclid Bonney’s (CRD Number 4899007) request for expungement of
the above-captioned arbitration (Occurrence Number 2286843) from CRD is denied with
prejudice.
2. Any and all claims for relief not specifically addressed herein, including any requests for
punitive damages and attorneys’ fees, are denied.
FEES
Pursuant to the Code, the following fees are assessed:
Filing Fees
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services assessed a filing fee* for each claim:
Initial Claim Filing Fee =$ 1,425.00
Expungement Filing Fee =$ 1,600.00
*The filing fee is made up of a non-refundable and a refundable portion.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 23-02063
Award Page 4 of 6
Member Fees
Member fees are assessed to each member firm that is a party in these proceedings or to the
member firm(s) that employed the associated person(s) at the time of the event(s) giving rise to
the dispute. Accordingly, as a party, Respondent MML is assessed the following:
Member Surcharge =$ 2,025.00
Member Process Fee =$ 3,875.00
Postponement Fees
Postponements granted during these proceedings for which fees were assessed or waived:
January 28-31, 2025, postponement requested by parties =$ 1,125.00
Total Postponement Fees =$ 1,125.00
The Panel has assessed $562.50 of the postponement fees jointly and severally to Claimants.
The Panel has assessed $562.50 of the postponement fees to Respondent Bonney.
Discovery-Related Motion Fees
Fees apply for each decision rendered on a discovery-related motion.
One (1) decision on a discovery-related motion on the papers =$ 600.00
with the Panel @ $600.00/decision
Claimants submitted One (1) discovery-related motion
Total Discovery-Related Motion Fees =$ 600.00
The Panel has assessed the total discovery-related motion fees jointly and severally to
Claimants.
Hearing Session Fees and Assessments
The Panel has assessed hearing session fees for each session conducted. A session is any
meeting between the parties and the Arbitrator(s), including a pre-hearing conference with the
Arbitrator(s), which lasts four (4) hours or less. Fees associated with these proceedings are:
Two (2) pre-hearing sessions with the Panel @ $1,125.00/session =$ 2,250.00
Pre-Hearing Conferences: November 9, 2023 1 session
February 27, 2024 1 session
Total Hearing Session Fees =$ 2,250.00
The Panel has assessed $1,687.50 of the hearing session fees jointly and severally to
Claimants.
The Panel has assessed $562.50 of the hearing session fees jointly and severally to
Respondents.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 23-02063
Award Page 5 of 6
All balances are payable to FINRA Dispute Resolution Services and are due upon receipt.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 23-02063
Award Page 6 of 6
ARBITRATION PANEL
David S. Eisenberg - Public Arbitrator, Presiding Chairperson
Loren Scott Buell - Public Arbitrator
David Alun Moody - Public Arbitrator
I, the undersigned Arbitrator, do hereby affirm that I am the individual described herein and who
executed this instrument, which is my award.
Concurring Arbitrators' Signatures
